macro_segments.title: Blood of a Carcass (Dam Neveilah): Impurity Debate and Resolution

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Rav Bibi's Story of the Dead Mule and the Revi'it of Blood
Challenge from Mishnah Eduyot: Reb Yehoshua and Reb Yehoshua ben Beteira Testify Dam Neveilah is Pure
The Story of Animals Slaughtered for Lions in the Stadium — Pilgrims Waded in Blood Without Concern
Rav Bibi's Silence Explained: He Was Too Anxious About His Livelihood to Answer
Resolution: Rav Yosef — Rebi Yehuda Ruled Like Beit Hillel That Dam Neveilah is Tamei
Reb Yosi b'Rebi Yehuda: Beit Hillel Only Declared Tamei with a Revi'it That Could Congeal to a Kezayit
Connection to Yesterday's Discussion of Shiurim and Rebi Yehuda's Role in Rebi's House

macro_segments.title: The Mishnah on Nedavat Nesachim: Permitted and Forbidden Quantities of Wine Donations

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: One May Not Donate Two or Five Log of Wine, But May Donate Three, Four, Six, and Above
The Basis: Wine Quantities Are Tied to Animal Sacrifice Denominations (Three, Four, Six Log)
The Question: Is There a Fixed Unit (Keva) for Nesachim Donations?
Abaye's Proof Attempt: The Six Shofarot Boxes in the Temple for Leftover Funds
Gemara's Response: Leftover Wine Can Be Combined with Others' Needs, No Separate Box Needed
Rava's Proof from the Verse 'Ezrach': One Can Bring Wine Independently, Minimum Three Log
Gemara Challenges: 'Yosef' Must Mean Five Log, Implying No Fixed Unit (Ein Keva)
Rav Ashi's Objection: The Mishnah Equates Five with Two, Implying Both Are Entirely Forbidden
Gemara Rejects Rav Ashi: The Two Cases Operate on Different Grounds
Abaye's New Question: If There Is Keva, What Happens When One Vows Eleven Log?
Teiku: The Question of Eleven Log Is Left Unresolved

macro_segments.title: Debate on Donating Oil (Shemen) by Itself: Rabbi Akiva vs. Rabbi Tarfon

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: Rabbi Akiva Says One May Donate Wine but Not Oil; Rabbi Tarfon Permits Oil
Rabbi Tarfon's Argument: Just as Wine Comes Obligatorily and Voluntarily, So Too Oil
Rabbi Akiva's Rebuttal: Wine Is Offered as a Separate Unit; Oil Never Comes Alone
Rava Infers from Both Opinions: One May Donate Minchat Nesachim (Flour-Oil Mixture) Daily
Gemara Clarifies: This Teaches That When Explicitly Specified, One May Donate the Nesachim Mincha Ratio

macro_segments.title: Partnership in Sacrifices: Why Two People Cannot Jointly Donate a Mincha — The Nefesh Derasha and Its Meaning

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah: Two People Cannot Jointly Donate a Single Isaron (Mincha), but May Jointly Give an Olah, Shelamim, or Bird
Derivation from 'Nefesh': The Torah Uses Soul Language by Mincha to Require Individual Donation
Baraita: All Sacrifices Can Be Brought in Partnership Except Mincha, Which Requires a Single Soul
Rabbi Yitzchak's Derasha: The Poor Person's Mincha Is Treated by God as if They Offered Their Very Soul
Why Does the Torah List Five Methods of Preparing the Mincha? The Parable of the Poor Friend and the King
Reflection: Why Partnership Is Forbidden for Mincha — The Dignity and Meaningfulness of the Poor Person's Gift
